name: Crown of Thorns
description: The Crown of Thorns is a sacred relic in Christian tradition, believed to be the crown placed on the head of Jesus Christ during his Passion, specifically before his crucifixion. According to the Gospels, the Roman soldiers mocked Jesus by placing this crown, woven from thorny branches, upon his head. This act was intended to ridicule his claim to be 'King of the Jews'. The crown is a symbol of immense suffering, humiliation, and ultimately, sacrifice. It represents the physical and emotional pain endured by Jesus for the sins of humanity. While the exact species of thorns used is unknown, the crown is deeply significant as a physical manifestation of Christ's suffering and a testament to his love and willingness to atone for the sins of the world. Throughout history, fragments of the crown have been venerated as relics, with the most famous being the purported 'Holy Crown' kept at Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris. The crown embodies themes of sacrifice, redemption, mockery, and divine kingship twisted and abused by human cruelty. Its existence, real or perceived, serves as a powerful reminder of the events leading to the crucifixion and the core tenets of Christian faith. The thorns themselves are seen as absorbing Christ's blood, making them intrinsically holy. The enduring reverence for the Crown of Thorns highlights the central role of suffering in Christian theology and the belief that through Christ's sacrifice, humanity can achieve salvation. It is a visceral symbol of the Passion and a tangible link to the events described in the Gospels.
subtitle: Symbol of Suffering and Sacrifice
origin: Woven by Roman soldiers to mock Jesus Christ before his crucifixion.
currentLocation: Relics claimed to be fragments are held in various churches; the most famous was held at Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris.
associatedHeroes: Saint Louis IX (King Louis IX of France)

keyMyths.title: The Mocking of Jesus
keyMyths.description: Roman soldiers placed the Crown of Thorns on Jesus' head, mocking his claim to be King of the Jews. This act of cruelty is a central event in the Passion narrative.

keyMyths.title: Relic Veneration
keyMyths.description: Fragments of the Crown of Thorns have been venerated as holy relics throughout Christian history. These relics are believed to possess spiritual power and are often displayed for public viewing.
keyMyths.source: Historical accounts of various churches and cathedrals

sources.author: Matthew
sources.description: Account of Jesus' Passion, including the crowning with thorns.
sources.text: Account of Jesus' Passion, including the crowning with thorns.

sources.author: Joan Carroll Cruz
sources.description: Examination of various Christian relics and their history.
